<security_assessment>

<details>
The PKGBUILD defines a `source` array with a local file reference (`file://DaVinci_Resolve_${pkgver}_Linux.zip`) and a local script (`davinci-control-panels-setup.sh`), both of which are expected to be present in the build directory. The only code evaluated at parse time (outside of functions) consists of static variable assignments and array declarations — there is no command substitution, function calls, or execution of external commands in the global scope. Therefore, sourcing this PKGBUILD for `makepkg --printsrcinfo` or downloading sources with `--nobuild` does not execute any potentially malicious code.

While the `prepare()` and `package()` functions contain significant logic (including extraction, `patchelf` usage, and udev rule installation), these stages are skipped under `--noprepare` and `--nobuild`, so they are irrelevant for this narrow audit. The local source is expected and safe to fetch — it simply requires manual acquisition of a vendor-distributed blob, a common practice for proprietary software like DaVinci Resolve. No network access or code execution occurs during the targeted commands.

The `file://` source and absence of code in `pkgver()` or the top-level scope ensures that parsing this PKGBUILD is safe.
</details>

Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD is being evaluated for safety when running `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare` and `makepkg --printsrcinfo`. These commands source the PKGBUILD's global scope and execute `pkgver()` if present (though there is no `pkgver()` function here), and fetch/extract sources defined in the `source` array.

In this file, the `source` array contains two entries: a local file `file://DaVinci_Resolve_${pkgver}_Linux.zip` and a script `davinci-control-panels-setup.sh`. The use of `file://` indicates that the large binary ZIP must be manually downloaded by the user and placed in the build directory — no automatic download from an external source occurs during build. This is a common practice for large proprietary binaries like DaVinci Resolve, which are not redistributable. The second source is a local helper script also expected to be present locally. Both are referenced securely and do not trigger network activity or code execution during sourcing.

There is no `pkgver()` function, so no dynamic version calculation occurs. The rest of the code (in `prepare()` and `package()`) will not be executed due to the `--noprepare` and `--nobuild` flags. Therefore, no decompression, patching, `patchelf` calls, or file manipulation happens during this phase. The top-level content of the PKGBUILD consists only of variable assignments and safe expansions (e.g., `${pkgver}` in string interpolation), with no command substitution, eval, or immediate code execution.

Thus, sourcing this PKGBUILD and downloading/extracting its sources (which in this case means verifying a pre-downloaded file) poses no security risk.
</details>
<summary>
No harmful execution during source fetch and parse
</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- No harmful execution during source fetch and parse[/]
